parts.name: Part 1
parts.instructions: You must answer this question. Write your answer in about 100 words.

tasks.lead: Read this email from Mr Okafor, the school librarian, and the notes you have made.

stimulus.head: EMAIL
stimulus.body:
From: Mr Okafor
Subject: Library reading project

Dear Students,

The library is starting a reading project next term. Each student will recommend one book for other students to read and will make a poster about it.

Which book would you like to recommend, and why? We also need helpers to organise the book display. Are you interested?

Mr Okafor

bullets: say which book you would recommend and why
describe what you would put on your poster
offer to help with the book display
ask one question about the project
tasks.write: Write your email to Mr Okafor.

parts.name: Part 2
parts.instructions: Answer one of these questions. Write your answer in about 100 words.

tasks.lead: You see this announcement in your school English-language magazine.

stimulus.title: PAPER BOOKS OR E-BOOKS?
stimulus.body: Some people love the feel of a real book, while others prefer reading on a screen. Which do you prefer, and why? Write an article and tell us. The best articles will be published next month.

bullets: whether you prefer paper books or e-books
what you are reading at the moment

tasks.lead: Your English teacher has asked you to write a story.
tasks.startSentence: Inside the old library book, Tariq found a letter written fifty years ago.

stimulus.body:
Your story must begin with this sentence:

Inside the old library book, Tariq found a letter written fifty years ago.
